description: The governance day starts as an assurance journey, not a leak hunt. Daniel is shown what needs confirming — a routine frontier-usage review, spend against budget, and a borderline field the knowledge graph caught and held local.

Field-level proof that member data stayed in-environment. Fields never route to the frontier model — tasks do. Every field resolves on the in-environment SLM; the frontier appears once, on non-PII inputs only.

The knowledge graph is the source of truth for what is sensitive and where it came from. It links a field to its source system and the policy node that governs it — classification by meaning, not keyword matching.

Two gates, evaluated in order. Safe to send: any field carrying PII stays in the SLM, always. Worth sending: a task reaches the frontier only when it needs reasoning or generation the SLM cannot do reliably. Both must pass.
Per-task routing and cost together. Every frontier call is deliberate and justified, and the saving against an all-frontier platform is measured, not asserted.

Continuous governance across the contact center: agent health, frontier task share and cost trend, with every agent action expandable to its context, chain of thought and the policy that drove it. Rendered in Ultra, not raw Grafana.
